session_d_pointers.description: G1097 ginōskō has 824 occurrences and 120 verses in export. Only a conscience-relevant subset has been analysed. Full corpus is far beyond Session B scope. Phase 2 independent research required before synthesis-level conclusions drawn from this term's full range.
G1492H eidō (see/perceive) has 396 occurrences and 119 verses in export. Only conscience-morally-relevant subset analysed. Phase 2 required.
G1492I eidō (know/understand) has 365 occurrences and 27 verses in export. Conscience-morally-relevant subset only. Phase 2 required.

H3627 ke.li has 325 occurrences and 225 verses in export. Classified extracted under broad-sweep approach for vessel/container metaphor relevance. The majority of the corpus is literal (weapons, bags, utensils) — not conscience vocabulary. Analysis must be confined strictly to the vessel-as-person / consecrated-vessel theological register. Phase 2 review required before any synthesis-level use of this term.

G1921 epiginōskō has 136 occurrences and 39 verses in export. Conscience-relevant subset (recognition, moral self-examination, spiritual perception) is a minority of the full corpus. Phase 2 required for full assessment.

Condemnation (katakrima/katakrinō family) is a candidate for a new registry entry. G2631 katakrima and root family G2632 katakrinō (to condemn), katakrisis, akatakritos, autokatakritos constitute a distinct inner-being vocabulary cluster addressing the judicial-moral verdict-state. Analytically distinct from guilt (moral fact of wrongdoing) and shame (relational/exposure experience). The condemnation/no-condemnation polarity is the structural framework of Romans 5-8 and is signalled by anchor verse Rom 8:1. ACTION: Check word_registry for existing condemnation entry. If absent, create new registry with anchor verse Rom 8:1 and run Session A extraction on G2632 family.

G0264 hamartanō (to sin, occ=241) has only 37 verses in the export — 15% coverage. Synthesis-level conclusions about the verbal form of sin in the NT cannot be drawn from this sample alone. Full verse set required before Phase 2 synthesis.

G0265 hamartēma (sin as discrete act, occ=26) has only 4 verses in the export — 15% coverage. The distinction between hamartia (sin as state/principle) and hamartēma (sin as individual act) is analytically significant. Conclusions about this distinction require Phase 2 full-verse verification.
G0093 (unrighteousness) — 214 occurrences, 24 verses in export (11% coverage). Analysis draws on available sample only. Full verse set required before synthesis-level conclusions on this term.
G0094 (unjust) — 101 occurrences, 11 verses in export (11% coverage). Analysis draws on available sample only. Full verse set required before synthesis-level conclusions on this term.
G0095 (unjustly) — 22 occurrences, 1 verses in export (5% coverage). Analysis draws on available sample only. Full verse set required before synthesis-level conclusions on this term.
G1342 (just) — 383 occurrences, 74 verses in export (19% coverage). Analysis draws on available sample only. Full verse set required before synthesis-level conclusions on this term.
G1345 (righteous act) — 128 occurrences, 10 verses in export (8% coverage). Analysis draws on available sample only. Full verse set required before synthesis-level conclusions on this term.
G1349 (condemnation) — 21 occurrences, 3 verses in export (14% coverage). Analysis draws on available sample only. Full verse set required before synthesis-level conclusions on this term.
G1556 (to avenge) — 71 occurrences, 6 verses in export (8% coverage). Analysis draws on available sample only. Full verse set required before synthesis-level conclusions on this term.
G1557 (vengeance) — 59 occurrences, 9 verses in export (15% coverage). Analysis draws on available sample only. Full verse set required before synthesis-level conclusions on this term.
G2118 (righteousness) — 21 occurrences, 1 verses in export (5% coverage). Analysis draws on available sample only. Full verse set required before synthesis-level conclusions on this term.
G2917 (judgment) — 232 occurrences, 27 verses in export (12% coverage). Analysis draws on available sample only. Full verse set required before synthesis-level conclusions on this term.
G2920 (judgment) — 253 occurrences, 47 verses in export (19% coverage). Analysis draws on available sample only. Full verse set required before synthesis-level conclusions on this term.
H4639H (deed: justice) — classified extracted_thin on sub-gloss label only. NO_VERSES in export. Independent verse research required before any synthesis conclusions drawn from this term.
H4639I (deed: judgement) — classified extracted_thin on sub-gloss label only. NO_VERSES in export. Independent verse research required before any synthesis conclusions drawn from this term.

H6419 (pālal, to pray, 84 occ) and H8605 (tepillāh, prayer, 77 occ) classified xref_prayer. Both carry a judicial-intercession dimension — prayer as address to the divine judge — directly relevant to justice. Session D must assess whether the prayer registry analysis adequately captures this usage, and if not, initiate a cross-registry pass.
